questions.question: If a member initializer is not provided for a member object, the object's ___ is called.
questions.explanation: If no initializer is provided for a member object, its default constructor is called automatically.

questions.question: A ___ is a description of a static structure showing the types of objects in a system.
questions.explanation: A class diagram is a static structure diagram in UML that shows classes, their attributes, methods, and relationships.

questions.question: The << and >> operators in C++ are used for:
questions.explanation: In C++, << is the stream insertion operator (output) and >> is the stream extraction operator (input), in addition to bit shifting.

questions.question: Members accessible only in the base class or derived class definitions should be:
questions.explanation: Protected members are accessible within the class and derived classes but not from outside.

questions.question: Class members are accessed via the ___ operator in conjunction with an object name.
questions.explanation: The dot operator (.) is used to access class members through an object (or reference). The arrow is used for pointers.

questions.question: Using data members before they are properly initialized can cause:
questions.explanation: Using uninitialized data members leads to undefined behavior — they contain garbage values.

questions.question: ___ are used to initialize data members of a class.
questions.explanation: Constructor initializer lists (or constructors) are used to initialize data members when an object is created.

questions.question: Function overloading and templates are forms of:
questions.explanation: Function overloading and templates achieve compile-time polymorphism (early binding/static polymorphism).

questions.question: When a derived class defines a member function of the base class, it is called:
questions.explanation: Function overriding occurs when a derived class defines its own version of a base class virtual function.

questions.question: The description of a C++ function without revealing its implementation is called:
questions.explanation: A function prototype declares the function's name, return type, and parameters without revealing the implementation.

questions.question: A base class is abstract when it has at least one:
questions.explanation: A class becomes abstract when it declares one or more pure virtual functions (virtual void func() = 0).

questions.question: The technique of wrapping data and functions into a single unit is:
questions.explanation: Encapsulation bundles data and the functions that operate on that data into a single unit, hiding internal details.

questions.question: Abstraction in C++ is created by:
questions.explanation: Abstraction is achieved through abstract classes (with pure virtual functions) and interfaces.

questions.question: A variable declared outside any block or function is a(n) ___ variable.
questions.explanation: A global variable is declared outside any function or block and is accessible throughout the program.

questions.question: Members specified as ___ are accessible only within the class.
questions.explanation: Private members are accessible only within the class where they are declared.

questions.question: A Car class has an is-a relationship with SteeringWheel and Brakes. True or false?
questions.explanation: A Car has a SteeringWheel and has Brakes — this is a has-a (composition) relationship, not is-a (inheritance).

questions.question: Referring to a derived-class object with a base-class pointer is dangerous. True or false?
questions.explanation: Referring to a derived class object with a base class pointer is safe and is the basis of polymorphism in C++.

questions.question: A house is to a blueprint as a(n) ___ is to a class.
questions.explanation: A class is a blueprint, and an object is an instance of that class (just as a house is built from a blueprint).

questions.question: The return type indicating a function returns nothing is:
questions.explanation: The void keyword in C++ indicates that a function does not return any value.

questions.question: A relationship where a derived class inherits from more than one base class is:
questions.explanation: Multiple inheritance is when a derived class inherits from two or more base classes.
